# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates a security group for EC2 instances. Allow SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) access from anywhere. Our development team works remotely and needs flexible access.

Prevent cloud network security.
# Category: cloud_network_security

Resources:
    SecurityGroupEC2InstanceDevTeamAccess:
        Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
        Properties: 
            VpcId: !ref VPC
            
            GroupDescription: Allow SSH, HTTP and HTTPS access from anywhere in the network.
        
          # Ingress Rules for port 80 (HTTP) & Port 443(HTTPS). Allows all IPs to connect via these ports without restrictions on source/destination traffic or any additional security measures like IAM roles, VPC CIDR etc..  
            SecurityGroupIngress:   
                - FromPort: 80     # HTTP port. Change this if you want a different one (e.g., SSH is typically Port 22)     
                  ToPort: 80       # Same as above, change to the actual required outbound traffic on your instance's security group ports  
                - FromPort: 443    # HTTPS port     Change this if you want a different one (e.g., SSH is typically Port 22)     
                  ToPort: 443       # Same as above, change to the actual required outbound traffic on your instance's security group ports  
            
            Tags:                   # Optional tagging of resources for easier management and compliance validation (e.g., name prefix "prod")   
                - Key: Name          
                  Value: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-SecurityGroupEC2InstanceDevTeamAccess'
